narrative:
First Warn Weather Headlines – Monday, May 11, 2026

Pleasant and seasonable weather today with sunshine and highs in the 70s.
Warmer temperatures return Tuesday ahead of a cold front.
A 30-70% chance of showers and thunderstorms develops Tuesday afternoon and evening.
Severe weather potential currently appears limited, though a few storms could produce gusty winds and small hail.
Cooler and drier air returns Wednesday into Thursday before another warm-up heading into the weekend.

After a cool and refreshing Mother’s Day weekend, temperatures will begin moderating across Missouri and Illinois today as high pressure remains parked across the bi-state region. Plenty of sunshine is expected through the day with afternoon highs climbing into the middle 70s, which is right around average for mid-May. Humidity levels will remain comfortable, making for an excellent day to spend outdoors, open the windows, or simply enjoy a quieter stretch of weather before changes arrive Tuesday.

Tonight, the center of high pressure slides toward the Ohio Valley, allowing southerly winds to gradually return across the region. This will help keep overnight temperatures milder compared to recent nights, with lows generally ranging from the upper 40s to middle 50s. The coolest spots will likely be across southeast Missouri and southwest Illinois where winds remain lighter for a longer period of time.

Tuesday will feature a noticeable warm-up as stronger southerly flow develops ahead of an approaching cold front. Afternoon temperatures are expected to rise into the lower 80s across much of the region, running roughly 5 to 10 degrees above normal for this time of year. While much of the day should remain dry, attention will turn toward the late afternoon and evening hours as showers and thunderstorms begin developing along the advancing cold front.

At this time, the overall severe weather threat appears fairly limited. The atmosphere will have strong wind shear in place, which can support organized storms, but instability and moisture remain somewhat lacking. Forecast models continue to suggest only modest energy available for thunderstorm development, mainly across portions of central and northeast Missouri. Because of this, widespread severe weather does not appear likely. However, a few stronger storms could still produce small hail and locally gusty winds, especially if storms are able to become surface-based during the evening hours. An isolated stronger storm or brief supercell cannot be completely ruled out, but confidence in that scenario remains low.

Behind the front, cooler and drier air settles back into the region Wednesday and Thursday. Afternoon highs will generally return to the 70s with lower humidity making conditions feel very comfortable once again. Overnight lows Wednesday night into Thursday morning will dip back into the 40s for many locations, especially in outlying rural areas.

Looking ahead toward late week and next weekend, warmer temperatures quickly rebound. Another period of well-above normal temperatures is expected beginning Friday and continuing through the weekend. Highs will likely return to the 80s area-wide, although occasional clouds or isolated rain chances could keep some communities slightly cooler at times. Right now, widespread 90-degree heat still appears unlikely, but the overall pattern certainly favors a much warmer stretch of weather heading into the second half of May.

Enjoy the sunshine today because spring is about to remind us once again that calm weather in the Midwest is usually just intermission.
